avoid: Do not install an unrelated package merely because its name resembles the missing module.
Do not use global or privileged installation when a project-local dependency is sufficient.
Do not claim recovery until the original application command succeeds.
learned_facts: Run the Node application once to reproduce the dependency failure.
Read the exact missing package or module name from the observed error.
Install that package in the current project under the active dependency policy.
Re-run the same application command to verify that the missing dependency failure is gone.

allowed: Inspect local application errors and project manifests.
Install the specifically observed missing package when the configured registry and package are approved.
Re-run the original local application verifier.
forbidden: Global, privileged, or unrelated dependency installation.
Treating installation output alone as proof of recovery.

evidence: Repeated Node missing-dependency repair episodes and deterministic procedure-rendering tests.
limitations: Package installation can execute lifecycle scripts and is not automatically safe.
The procedure does not select package versions or authorize network access.

title: Recover a Node application with a missing dependency
